member.name: T:Fusion.PluginCodeExportSettingsAttribute
member.summary:
Use this attribute to control how the attributed code will be exported for use in the plugin.
Typical behaviour for the exporter is to export all 's 's and 's. if however this is not desired you can add this attribute with the option to skip it from the export.
This attribute can also be added to your own types with the option to include those types in the export.

member.name: T:Fusion.CloudServices
member.summary: Responsible to deal with LoadBalancingClient Events

Responsible for dealing and managing the API used to communicate with the Photon Cloud. This also includes: - Send/Reply to Protocol Messages - Query for Reflexive Information - Perform NAT Punchthrough - Manage the Realtime client - Respond to/deal with Photon Cloud events

member.name: P:Fusion.CloudServices.RoundTripTime
member.summary:
Retrieve the network latency between the local peer and the Photon Cloud, along with the last calculated Round Trip Time in seconds. Only provide valid values if the peer is connected to the session; otherwise, return (0, 0).
This data is independent of which Fusion Game Mode the peer is running, as it considers only the connection between the local peer and the Photon Cloud.
Use this information for debugging or development purposes only.
member.returns: A tuple with the Average RTT and the Last RTT with the Photon Cloud

member.name: M:Fusion.CloudServices.OnInternalConnectionAttempt(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean@,Fusion.Sockets.NetAddress@)
member.summary:
Callback fire on every connection attempt with a remote Server.
It is used while trying to hole-punch the remote server and enables the manager to swap the target endpoint in between attempts. This is necessary to maintain a flow of attempts even if we exchange the local/public/relay endpoints
member.param: Current attempt number
Max number of attempts
Flag if target EndPoint should change
New target EndPoint

Base class for Fusion network components, which are associated with a .

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkBehaviour.ChangeDetector
member.summary: Change detector for a NetworkBehaviour
member.remarks: This class is used to detect changes in a NetworkBehaviour. It can be used to detect changes in a NetworkBehaviour between two snapshots, or between the current state and a snapshot.

member.summary: Enum representing the source of a NetworkBehaviour's state.

member.summary: The state is from a previous snapshot of the NetworkBehaviour.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkBehaviour.ChangeDetector.Source.SnapshotTo
member.summary: The state is from a future snapshot of the NetworkBehaviour.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkBehaviour.ChangeDetector.Source.SnapshotNearest
member.summary: The state is based on whether the interpolation alpha is above or below 0.5. Below 0.5 we use the previous snapshot, above 0.5 we use the future snapshot of the NetworkBehaviour.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourBufferInterpolator.Valid
member.summary: A value indicating whether this interpolator is valid. An interpolator is considered valid if it has successfully retrieved the "from" and "to" buffers.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId

member.summary: The NetworkId of the object this behaviour belongs to.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.Behaviour
member.summary: The identifier for the behaviour within the object.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.IsValid
member.summary: Checks if the NetworkBehaviourId is valid. A NetworkBehaviourId is valid if its Object is valid and its Behaviour is non-negative.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.None
member.summary: Returns a new NetworkBehaviourId with default values.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.Equals(Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId)
member.summary: Checks if this NetworkBehaviourId is equal to another NetworkBehaviourId. Two NetworkBehaviourIds are equal if their Objects and Behaviours are equal.
member.param: The other NetworkBehaviourId to compare with.
member.returns: True if the NetworkBehaviourIds are equal, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks if this NetworkBehaviourId is equal to another object. The object is considered equal if it is a NetworkBehaviourId and its Object and Behaviour are equal to this NetworkBehaviourId's.
member.param: The object to compare with.
member.returns: True if the object is a NetworkBehaviourId and is equal to this, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns a hash code for this NetworkBehaviourId. The hash code is computed based on the Object's hash code and the Behaviour.
member.returns: A hash code for this NetworkBehaviourId.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of the NetworkBehaviourId. The string representation is in the format: [Object:{Object}, Behaviour:{Behaviour}].
member.returns: A string representation of the NetworkBehaviourId.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.op_Equality(Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId,Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId)
member.summary: Determines whether two NetworkBehaviourId instances are equal. Two NetworkBehaviourId instances are considered equal if their Objects and Behaviours are equal.
member.param: The first NetworkBehaviourId to compare.
The second NetworkBehaviourId to compare.
member.returns: True if the NetworkBehaviourId instances are equal, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId.op_Inequality(Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId,Fusion.NetworkBehaviourId)
member.summary: Determines whether two NetworkBehaviourId instances are not equal. Two NetworkBehaviourId instances are considered not equal if their Objects or Behaviours are not equal.
member.param: The first NetworkBehaviourId to compare.
The second NetworkBehaviourId to compare.
member.returns: True if the NetworkBehaviourId instances are not equal, false otherwise.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils
member.summary: This static class provides utility methods for working with NetworkBehaviour objects.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils.InvokeRpc
member.summary: A static field that determines whether to invoke RPC (Remote Procedure Call). When set to true, RPCs are invoked. When set to false, RPCs are not invoked. Default value is false.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils.GetCustomSharedModeReadOnlyOffsets(System.Type,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves words offsets of properties with set.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils.GetWordCount(Fusion.NetworkBehaviour)
member.summary: Retrieves the word count for a given NetworkBehaviour. If the NetworkBehaviour has a dynamic word count, it is returned. Otherwise, the static word count for the type of the NetworkBehaviour is returned.
member.param: The NetworkBehaviour for which to retrieve the word count.
member.returns: The word count for the given NetworkBehaviour.
member.exception: Thrown when the dynamic word count or the static word count is negative.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils.HasStaticWordCount(System.Type)
member.summary: Checks if a given type has a static word count. A type has a static word count if it is a subclass of NetworkBehaviour and its word count is non-negative.
member.param: The type to check.
member.returns: True if the type has a static word count, false otherwise.
member.exception: Thrown when the provided type is not a subclass of NetworkBehaviour.
Thrown when the provided type does not have a weaved attribute.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkBehaviourUtils.GetStaticWordCount(System.Type)
member.summary: Retrieves the static word count for a given type. If the word count for the type has not been computed yet, it is computed and stored.
member.param: The type for which to retrieve the static word count.
member.returns: The static word count for the given type.
member.exception: Thrown when the provided type is not a subclass of NetworkBehaviour.
Thrown when the provided type does not have a weaved attribute or its word count is negative.

member.exception: Thrown when the NetworkBehaviour object is not initialized.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator
member.summary: A component for synchronizing the Animator controller state from the State Authority to network proxies. Requires a Unity Animator component, and a component. NOTE: Animator Root Motion is not compatible with re-simulation and prediction.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.DynamicWordCount
member.summary: Gets the dynamic word count for the NetworkMecanimAnimator.
member.value: The dynamic word count, which is the maximum of the current total words and the runtime counts, if the application is playing.
member.exception: Thrown when this property is accessed outside of playing.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.Animator
member.summary: The Animator being synced. If unset, will attempt to find one on this GameObject.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.ApplyTiming
member.summary: The source of the State which is applied in Render.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.SyncSettings
member.summary: Flags controlling which Mecanim data will be synced.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.StateHashes
member.summary: States found in the current AnimatorController, converted to hashes.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.TriggerHashes
member.summary: Triggers found in the current AnimatorController, converted to hashes.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.TotalWords
member.summary: The number of words allocated per snapshot for serialization of the Animator. (One Word is 32 bits, or 4 bytes.)

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator._animatorData
member.summary: Animator data captured on the first invocation of EnsureInitialized().

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator._isInitialized
member.summary: Have and been initialized?

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.BITS_PER_BOOL
member.summary: To ensure triggers are synced consistently, 1 bit is used for true/false and 3 more are used to differentiate the state (these are incremented with every change to the underlying bool).

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.AnimatorData
member.summary: Capture of Animator data.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.SetTrigger(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Queues a SetTrigger() call for the associated Animator on the State Authority. Call this instead of Animator.SetTrigger() for the State Authority to ensure that triggers are captured. On State Authority, this call will defer the SetTrigger() pass-through to the Animator until FixedUpdateNetwork() is called, where all queued triggers will be executed (this is to ensure tick agreement between server and clients).
member.param: Trigger hash to set

Will call Animator.SetTrigger() immediately on the InputAuthority. If false, SetTrigger() will not be called on the Input Authority at all and Animator.SetTrigger() should be called explicitly as needed.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkMecanimAnimator.SetTrigger(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Queues a SetTrigger() call for the associated Animator on the State Authority. Call this instead of Animator.SetTrigger() for the State Authority to ensure that triggers are captured. On State Authority, this call will defer the SetTrigger() pass-through to the Animator until FixedUpdateNetwork() is called, where all queued triggers will be executed (this is to ensure tick agreement between server and clients).
member.param: Trigger name to set

Will call Animator.SetTrigger() immediately on the InputAuthority. If false, SetTrigger() will not be called on the Input Authority at all and Animator.SetTrigger() should be called explicitly as needed.

member.name: T:Fusion.SimulationBehaviour
member.summary: Base class for a Fusion aware Behaviour (derived from UnityEngine.MonoBehavour). If a SimulationBehaviour is found on a game object during the runner initialisation, the SimulationBehaviour is automatically registered. Objects derived from this object can be associated with a and using NetworkRunner.AddGlobal().

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this instance can receive simulation callbacks.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkTransform.AutoUpdateAreaOfInterestOverride
member.summary: Determines if parent changes should automatically call , and assign the parent as the override. Default is true, as you typically will want player interest in this object to reflect player interest in the nested parent object. For example, if a player is carrying an nested Object, players should only see that carried Object if they see the player. Additionally, AOI works in world space, and NetworkTransform operates in local space, so any AOI position values of nested Objects will ALWAYS be invalid, so nested Objects should always have their AOI Override set to a non-nested Object.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkTransform._autoAOIOverride

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkTransform.Teleport(System.Nullable{UnityEngine.Vector3},System.Nullable{UnityEngine.Quaternion})
member.summary:
Teleport the object to a new position. Network the Teleport event and disable interpolation so the object snaps to the new position.
When and are true the RigidBody.position is adjusted in addition to Transform.position. Otherwise just the Transform.position is changed suspending interpolation between the previous tick state and the current tick state in Render(), on this peer and all remote peers.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkTransform.CustomErrorDetection
member.summary:
Provide a custom forecast error detection heuristic to override the default behaviour.
This heuristic is used to detect stalls in the forecasting logic and can be used to trigger a hard reset of the forecast system.
When null, the default heuristic is used which detects persistent error states where the error direction is consistent but correction progress is insufficient.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkTransformHelpers
member.summary: Class of useful helpers for the NetworkTransform.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkTransformHelpers.DefaultStalledErrorCorrectionDetection(Fusion.NetworkTransform,System.Single,Fusion.AbstractPhysicsBody,UnityEngine.Vector3,UnityEngine.Vector3,Fusion.KinematicSnapshot@,Fusion.KinematicSnapshot@)
member.summary:
This is the default stall detection heuristic that the forecast physics uses.
It carries out two checks
1. It calculates how much the error vectors have changed, from the last frame to this frame
2. It calculates how much of the last frames error was corrected
It then compares these values against the thresholds supplied in the PhysicsSettings on the network transform. Namely and
This default heuristic can be replaced with custom implementation by setting on a
Note: An alternative heuristic has been provided that works similarly to this but uses normalized vectors
member.param: The network transform being checked
The timestep of the physics system
The physics body being forecasted
The world space position captured last FixedUpdate
The desired extrapolation position captured last FixedUpdate
The local physics data for this object for this FixedUpdate
The desired extrapolated physics data for this object for this FixedUpdate

summary.para: Error detection heuristic that detects persistent stuck states. differs from the standard implementation in that it is normalized and time-step independent and tuned via 3 fractional or precentage based thresholds

Evaluates three conditions: 1. Error direction similarity - are current and previous errors pointing the same way? 2. Correction progress - are we making progress closing the gap? 3. Error persistence - is the error magnitude staying the same or growing?

When all three indicate a problem (consistent error direction, no progress, error not shrinking), returns a positive score indicating error. Otherwise returns a negative score indicating healthy correction.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkTransformHelpers.NormalizedStallHeuristic.#ctor(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)

summary.para: Constructs a
Once an instance has been made the property can be assigned to the delegate on a network transform to override the default stall heurstics algorithm used

member.param: Error distances below this value will return -1f, the error is too small to care
What proportion of the error from the previous frame needs to match this frame to be considered stalled?
What proportion of last frames error needs to have been corrected this frame to be considered not stalled?
How much does this frames error need to have shrunk by, compared with last frames error to not be considered stalled?

member.summary: Per object option to control Forecast Physics. Only effective when Forecast Physics is enabled by

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.SetForecastDisabledProxyRigidbodiesToKinematic
member.summary: When true, the rigidbody will be set to kinematic for proxy objects when Forecast Physics is disabled. This lets a Host or Dedicated server run physics which are in remote time and 'view only' from the point of view of clients. Setting this to false is for compatibility with Fusion 2.0 behaviour and is otherwise not recommended.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.SetPhysicsInterpolationToDisabledWhenKinematic
member.summary: When true, physics interpolation will be disabled for the Rigidbody on Forecast Physics objects which are set to kinematic. The original state is restored when kinematic is disabled. This prevents the physics interpolation interfering with object motion. Set to false to handle this explicitly in game code and have Fusion not change the physics interpolation settings.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.DebugPhysics
member.summary: Draw physics debug gizmos.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.GravityForecast
member.summary: Whether we apply gravity to the forecast.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MaxExtrapolationTime
member.summary: Maximum extrapolation time clients are allowed to do. Higher values means extrapolating more.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MinLinearDetectedError
member.summary: Error must be over this value (metres) for correction to be applied. Errors below this threshold are not considered

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MinAngularDetectedError
member.summary: Error must be over this value (degrees) for correction to be applied. Errors below this threshold are not considered

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MaxLinearError
member.summary: Linear error above this threshold will force an immediate move to the remote physics state. Zero means no limit.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MaxAngularError
member.summary: Angular error above this threshold will force a immediate move to the remote physics state. Zero means no limit.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.LowCorrectionProgressThreshold
member.summary: If correction progress is below this accrue time towards a reset, in metres.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.HighErrorSimilarityThreshold
member.summary: If current error direction and magnitude dot previous error direction and magnitude is above this accrue time towards a reset. This value roughly represents how little the direction of the linear error has changed, and how big the error is. Roughly metres squared.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MaxErrorTotalTime
member.summary: The maximum amount of time in seconds that the physics body is allowed to accrue errors that are not considered to be corrected before performing an immediate move to the remote physics state.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MaxRemoteSleepIgnoreTime
member.summary: The maximum amount of time in seconds that the physics body will ignore remote state informing that the body should be sleeping. Higher values give local physics more time to try to reach the sleeping state before an immediate move is required.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.ErrorCorrectionType
member.summary: Correction type to be applied on the physics body. [Velocity] is recommended.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.LinearVelCorrectionMul
member.summary: A scale factor applied to the correction velocity if [Velocity] or [PositionRotation] is the [Error Correction Type]. Higher values means the body will be corrected faster, but can increase overshoot

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.AngularVelCorrectionMul
member.summary: Coefficient used when extrapolating the angular velocity. Higher values will result in a bigger extrapolated velocity. See

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.PositionCorrectionLerp
member.summary: Lerp object position from local to extrapolated (Range 0..1). Only used if the Error Correction Type is [Position Rotation]. Applied in FixedUpdate()

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.RotationCorrectionLerp
member.summary: Lerp object rotation from local to extrapolated (Range 0..1) Applied in FixedUpdate()

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.Spring
member.summary: The intensity of the error correction, increase this value to make the error be corrected faster. If overshooting try increasing .

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.Damper
member.summary: The resistance of the correction oscillation, increase this value to increase the resistance of the spring correction and control overshooting. Keep it bellow the value.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.MinImpactfulCollisionAlignment
member.summary:
Minimum collision alignment required to trigger local physics simulation. 0 represents a collision movement parallel to the surface (glancing) 1 represents a collision movement perpendicular to the surface (head-on)
Setting a value of 0 will treat all collisions as impactful
This value is cos(theta) where theta is the angle between the objects velocity and the collisions normal.
When a collision is detected above this threshold the impact correction timer starts and for further details

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.ImpactStartCorrectionTime
member.summary: How long after an impact (OnCollisionEnter called) do we apply a correction. Between the impact and this time no local correction is applied.

member.name: F:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.ImpactCorrectionTimeComplete
member.summary: After we lerp from no correction to normal correction at this time.

member.name: M:Fusion.PhysicsSettings.#ctor
member.summary: Physics settings.

member.name: T:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities
member.summary: Tools to replace GetComponent variants that respects nested objects. These are used to find components of a NetworkedObjects without also finding components that belong to parent or child NetworkedObjects.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.EnsureRootComponentExists``2(UnityEngine.Transform)
member.summary: Ensures that a component of type T exists on the root object of the provided transform. If a component of type T does not exist, it is added. The search for the root object stops if a component of type TStopOn is found.
member.typeparam: The type of component to ensure exists on the root object.
The type of component that stops the search for the root object.
member.param: The transform to start the search from.
member.returns: The component of type T on the root object. Returns null if no root object is found.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetParentComponent``1(UnityEngine.Transform)
member.summary: Find T on supplied transform or any parent. Unlike GetComponentInParent, GameObjects do not need to be active to be found.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentsInParents``1(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0})
member.summary: Returns all T found between the child transform and its root. Order in List from child to parent, with the root/parent most being last.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentInChildren``2(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds the first component of type T in the children of the provided transform, stopping the search if a component of type TStopOn is found.
member.typeparam: The type of component to find.
The type of component that stops the search.
member.param: The transform to start the search from.
Whether to include inactive game objects in the search.
member.returns: The first component of type T found. Returns null if no component is found.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentInParent``2(UnityEngine.Transform)
member.summary: Same as GetComponentInParent, but will always include inactive objects in search. Will also stop recursing up the hierarchy when the StopOnT is found.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentInParents``2(UnityEngine.Transform)
member.summary: Finds the first component of type T in the parents of the provided transform, stopping the search if a component of type TStopOn is found.
member.typeparam: The type of component to find.
The type of component that stops the search.
member.param: The transform to start the search from.
member.returns: The first component of type T found in the parents. Returns null if no component is found or a component of type TStopOn is found.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentsInParents``2(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0})
member.summary: Finds components of type T on supplied transform, and every parent above that node, inclusively stopping on node StopT component.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentsInChildren``2(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Same as GetComponentsInChildren, but will not recurse into children with component of the StopT type.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentsInChildren``1(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0},System.Boolean,System.Type[])
member.summary: Same as GetComponentsInChildren, but will not recurse into children with any component of the types in the stopOn array.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.GetNestedComponentsInChildren``3(UnityEngine.Transform,System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0})
member.summary: Same as GetComponentsInChildren, but will not recurse into children with component of the StopT type.
member.typeparam: Cast found components to this type. Typically Component, but any other class/interface will work as long as they are assignable from SearchT.
Find components of this class or interface type.
When this component is found, no further recursing will be performed on that node.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.FindObjectsOfTypeInOrder``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Find All instances of Component type in a scene. Attempts to respect the hierarchy of the scene objects to produce a more deterministic order. This is a slower operation, and does produce garbage collection.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.FindObjectsOfTypeInOrder``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Find All instances of Component type in a scene. Attempts to respect the hierarchy of the scene objects to produce a more deterministic order. This is a slower operation which should not be run every update.
member.typeparam: The type being searched for.
member.param: Scene to search.
Supplied list that will be populated by this find.
Whether results should include inactive components.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.FindObjectsOfTypeInOrder``2(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Find All instances of Component type in a scene. Attempts to respect the hierarchy of the scene objects to produce a more deterministic order. This is a slow operation, and does produce garbage collection.
member.typeparam: The type being searched for.
Casts all found objects to this type, and returns collection of this type. Objects that fail cast are excluded.
member.param: Scene to search.
Whether results should include inactive components.

member.name: M:Fusion.NestedComponentUtilities.FindObjectsOfTypeInOrder``2(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Collections.Generic.List{``1},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Find All instances of Component type in a scene. Attempts to respect the hierarchy of the scene objects to produce a more deterministic order. This is a slower operation and should not be run every update.
member.typeparam: Type being searched for.
Type to cast found objects to.
member.param: Scene to search.
Supplied list that will be filled with found objects.
Whether results should include inactive components.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRNG.Next(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a random int within [minInclusive, maxExclusive) (range is exclusive). If minInclusive and maxExclusive are equal, then the "exclusive rule" is ignored and minInclusive will be returned. If minInclusive is greater than maxExclusive, then the numbers are automatically swapped.

member.name: T:Fusion.HitboxManager
member.summary:
Entry point for lag compensated queries, which maintains a history buffer, and provides lag compensated raycast and overlap methods. Singleton instance is accessible through the property Runner.LagCompensation.
Usage - Call any of the following methods: HitboxManager.Raycast()
HitboxManager.RaycastAll()
HitboxManager.PositionRotation()
HitboxManager.OverlapSphere()
These methods use the history buffer to perform a query against a state consistent with how the indicated perceived them locally.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxManager.BVHDepth
member.summary: Debug data from Broadphase BVH (tree depth).

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxManager.BVHNodes
member.summary: Debug data from Broadphase BVH (total nodes count).

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxManager.TotalHitboxes
member.summary: Debug data from lag compensation history (registered count).

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxManager.DrawInfo
member.summary: Debug data used to draw the BVH nodes and the lag compensation history.

member.name: M:Fusion.HitboxManager.Raycast(UnityEngine.Vector3,UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single,Fusion.PlayerRef,Fusion.LagCompensatedHit@,System.Int32,Fusion.HitOptions,UnityEngine.QueryTriggerInteraction,Fusion.LagCompensation.PreProcessingDelegate)
member.summary: Performs a lag-compensated raycast query against all registered hitboxes. If the or flag is indicated, query will also include static colliders, Unity colliders are recommended for static geometry, rather than Hitboxes.
member.param: Raycast origin, in world-space
Raycast direction, in world-space
Raycast length
Player who "owns" this raycast. Used by the server to find the exact hitbox snapshots to check against.
Raycast results will be filled in here.
Only objects with matching layers will be checked against.
Opt-in flags to compute with sub-tick accuracy () and/or to include PhysX () or Box2D ().
Trigger interaction behavior when also querying PhysX.

Delegate to pre-process HitboxRoots found in the broad-phase resolution of the query. Roots removed from the list will not be processed any further. Roots that remain on the candidates collection will be normally processed and fitting colliders will be evaluated in the query narrow-phase resolution. Hitbox collider indices added to the processed set will be evaluated in the narrow-phase regardless of further root processing steps (e.g. layer mask match).
member.returns: True if something is hit

member.name: M:Fusion.HitboxManager.Raycast(UnityEngine.Vector3,UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Single},Fusion.LagCompensatedHit@,System.Int32,Fusion.HitOptions,UnityEngine.QueryTriggerInteraction,Fusion.LagCompensation.PreProcessingDelegate)
member.summary: Performs a lag-compensated raycast query against all registered hitboxes. If the or flag is indicated, query will also include static colliders, Unity colliders are recommended for static geometry, rather than Hitboxes.
member.param: Raycast origin, in world-space
Raycast direction, in world-space
Raycast length
Simulation tick number to use as the time reference for the lag compensation (use this for server AI, and similar).

Simulation tick number to use as the time reference for the lag compensation. If provided, must be combined with the parameter for interpolation between and . If is included on , this query will be resolved against hitbox colliders interpolated between the specified ticks. Otherwise, only one of the two ticks will be considered, according to the rounded value of .

Interpolation value when querying between and . If is included on , this query will be resolved against hitbox colliders interpolated between the specified ticks. Otherwise, only one of the two ticks will be considered, according to the rounded alpha value.
Raycast results will be filled in here.
Only objects with matching layers will be checked against.
Opt-in flags to compute with sub-tick accuracy () and/or to include PhysX () or Box2D ().
Trigger interaction behavior when also querying PhysX.

Delegate to pre-process HitboxRoots found in the broad-phase resolution of the query. Roots removed from the list will not be processed any further. Roots that remain on the candidates collection will be normally processed and fitting colliders will be evaluated in the query narrow-phase resolution. Hitbox collider indices added to the processed set will be evaluated in the narrow-phase regardless of further root processing steps (e.g. layer mask match).
member.returns: True if something is hit

member.name: M:Fusion.HitboxManager.RaycastAll(UnityEngine.Vector3,UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single,Fusion.PlayerRef,System.Collections.Generic.List{Fusion.LagCompensatedHit},System.Int32,System.Boolean,Fusion.HitOptions,UnityEngine.QueryTriggerInteraction,Fusion.LagCompensation.PreProcessingDelegate)
member.summary: Performs a lag-compensated raycast query against all registered hitboxes. If the or flag is indicated, query will also include static colliders, Unity colliders are recommended for static geometry, rather than Hitboxes. Important: results are NOT sorted by distance.
member.param: Raycast origin, in world-space
Raycast direction, in world-space
Raycast length
Player who "owns" this raycast. Used by the server to find the exact hitbox snapshots to check against.
List to be filled with hits (both hitboxes and/or static colliders, if included).
Only objects with matching layers will be checked against.
Opt-in flags to compute with sub-tick accuracy () and/or to include PhysX () or Box2D ().
Clear list of hits before filling with new ones (defaults to true).
Trigger interaction behavior when also querying PhysX.

Delegate to pre-process HitboxRoots found in the broad-phase resolution of the query. Roots removed from the list will not be processed any further. Roots that remain on the candidates collection will be normally processed and fitting colliders will be evaluated in the query narrow-phase resolution. Hitbox collider indices added to the processed set will be evaluated in the narrow-phase regardless of further root processing steps (e.g. layer mask match).
member.returns: total number of hits

member.summary: Performs a lag-compensated raycast query against all registered hitboxes. If the or flag is indicated, query will also include static colliders, Unity colliders are recommended for static geometry, rather than Hitboxes. Important: results are NOT sorted by distance.
member.param: Raycast origin, in world-space
Raycast direction, in world-space
Raycast length
Simulation tick number to use as the time reference for the lag compensation (use this for server AI, and similar).

Simulation tick number to use as the time reference for the lag compensation. If provided, must be combined with the parameter for interpolation between and . If is included on , this query will be resolved against hitbox colliders interpolated between the specified ticks. Otherwise, only one of the two ticks will be considered, according to the rounded value of .

Interpolation value when querying between and . If is included on , this query will be resolved against hitbox colliders interpolated between the specified ticks. Otherwise, only one of the two ticks will be considered, according to the rounded alpha value.
List to be filled with hits (both hitboxes and/or static colliders, if included).
Only objects with matching layers will be checked against.
Opt-in flags to compute with sub-tick accuracy () and/or to include PhysX () or Box2D ().
Clear list of hits before filling with new ones (defaults to true).
Trigger interaction behavior when also querying PhysX.

Delegate to pre-process HitboxRoots found in the broad-phase resolution of the query. Roots removed from the list will not be processed any further. Roots that remain on the candidates collection will be normally processed and fitting colliders will be evaluated in the query narrow-phase resolution. Hitbox collider indices added to the processed set will be evaluated in the narrow-phase regardless of further root processing steps (e.g. layer mask match).
member.returns: total number of hits

member.name: T:Fusion.HitboxRoot
member.summary:
Root group container. Manages registering/unregistering hitboxes with the group, and defines the broadphase geometry for the group.
Broadphase is the initial rough query used by raycasts/overlaps/etc to find potential hit candidates, which are then used in the final narrowphase query.

member.name: T:Fusion.HitboxRoot.ConfigFlags
member.summary: Set of configuration options for a Hitbox Root behaviour.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.ConfigFlags.ReinitializeHitboxesBeforeRegistration
member.summary: If the collection of hitboxes under a given root should be re-initialized before the Root is registered in a hitbox snapshot. If disabled, the hitboxes will be used as configured in edit-time.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.ConfigFlags.IncludeInactiveHitboxes
member.summary: If Hitboxes on inactive Game Objects should be registered under this root upon initialization.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.ConfigFlags.Legacy
member.summary: Set of configuration flags that replicate the behaviour as it was before the flag options were added.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.ConfigFlags.Default
member.summary: Ser of configuration flags with the default behaviour, suitable for most use-cases.

member.name: P:Fusion.HitboxRoot.HitboxRootActive
member.summary: Get or set the state of this HitboxRoot. For a hitbox to be hit by lag-compensated queries, both it and its HitboxRoot must be active.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.MAX_HITBOXES
member.summary: The max number of hitboxes allowed under the same root.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.Config
member.summary: Set of configuration options for this Hitbox Root behaviour. Check the API documentation for more details on what each flag represents.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.BroadRadius
member.summary:
The radius of the broadphase bounding sphere for this group. Used by to insert/update lag compensated NetworkObjects into its BVH (bounding volume hierarchy) data structure. Be sure this radius encompasses all children components (including their full ranges of animation motion). We plan to offer an option to dynamically compute the bounding volume, but the performance trade of will still favor a hand-crafted radius.
Broadphase is the initial rough query used by raycasts/overlaps/etc to find potential hit candidates, which are then used in the final narrowphase query.

member.name: F:Fusion.HitboxRoot.Offset
member.summary:
Local-space offset of the broadphase bounding sphere from its transform position.
Adjust the and until the sphere gizmo (shown in the Unity Scene window) encompasses all children components (including their full ranges of animation motion).
Broadphase is the initial rough query used by raycasts/overlaps/etc to find potential hit candidates, which are then used in the final narrowphase query.

member.name: T:Fusion.FloatUtils
member.summary: Provides utility methods for compressing and decompressing float values.

member.name: F:Fusion.FloatUtils.DEFAULT_ACCURACY
member.summary: Default accuracy for float compression and decompression. That is, the number of bits after the decimal point.

member.name: M:Fusion.FloatUtils.Compress(System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Compresses a float value.
member.param: The float value to compress.
The accuracy to use for compression. That is, the number of bits after the decimal point. Defaults to DEFAULT_ACCURACY.
member.returns: The compressed float value.

member.name: M:Fusion.FloatUtils.Decompress(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Decompresses a compressed float value.
member.param: The compressed float value to decompress.
The accuracy to use for decompression. That is, the number of bits after the decimal point. Defaults to DEFAULT_ACCURACY.
member.returns: The decompressed float value.

member.name: M:Fusion.FloatUtils.DecompressStoreAsInt(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Decompresses a compressed float value.
member.param: The compressed float value to decompress.
The accuracy to use for decompression. That is, the number of bits after the decimal point. Defaults to DEFAULT_ACCURACY.
member.returns: The decompressed float value.

member.name: T:Fusion.InterpolatedErrorCorrectionSettings
member.summary: A set of parameters that tune the interpolated correction of prediction error on transform data.

summary.para: A factor with dimension of 1/s (Hz) that works as a lower limit for how much of the accumulated prediction error is corrected every frame. This factor affects both the position and the rotation correction. Suggested values are greater than zero and smaller than MaxRate.
E.g.: MinRate = 3, rendering delta time = (1/60)s: at least 5% (3 * 1/60) of the accumulated error will be corrected on this rendered frame.

This threshold might not be respected if the resultant correction magnitude is below the PosMinCorrection or above the PosTeleportDistance, for the position error, or above the RotTeleportRadians, for the rotation error.

A factor with dimension of 1/s (Hz) that works as a upper limit for how much of the accumulated prediction error is corrected every frame. This factor affects both the position and the rotation correction. Suggested values are greater than MinRate and smaller than half of a target rendering rate.
E.g.: MaxRate = 15, rendering delta time = (1/60)s: at maximum 25% (15 * 1/60) of the accumulated error will be corrected on this rendered frame.

This threshold might not be respected if the resultant correction magnitude is below the PosMinCorrection or above the PosTeleportDistance, for the position error, or above the RotTeleportRadians, for the rotation error.

The reference for the magnitude of the accumulated position error, in meters, at which the position error will be corrected at the MinRate. Suggested values are greater than PosMinCorrection and smaller than PosBlendEnd.

In other words, if the magnitude of the accumulated error is equal to or smaller than this threshold, it will be corrected at the MinRate. If, instead, the magnitude is between this threshold and PosBlendEnd, the error is corrected at a rate between MinRate and MaxRate, proportionally. If it is equal to or greater than PosBlendEnd, it will be corrected at the MaxRate.

Note: as the factor is expressed in distance units (meters), it might need to be scaled proportionally to the overall scale of objects in the scene and speeds at which they move, which are factors that affect the expected magnitude of prediction errors.

The reference for the magnitude of the accumulated position error, in meters, at which the position error will be corrected at the MaxRate. Suggested values are greater than PosBlendStart and smaller than PosTeleportDistance.

In other words, if the magnitude of the accumulated error is equal to or greater than this threshold, it will be corrected at the MaxRate. If, instead, the magnitude is between PosBlendStart and this threshold, the error is corrected at a rate between MinRate and MaxRate, proportionally. If it is equal to or smaller than PosBlendStart, it will be corrected at the MinRate.

Note: as the factor is expressed in distance units (meters), it might need to be scaled proportionally to the overall scale of objects in the scene and speeds at which they move, which are factors that affect the expected magnitude of prediction errors.
The value, in meters, that represents the minimum magnitude of the accumulated position error that will be corrected in a single frame, until it is fully corrected.

This setting has priority over the resultant correction rate, i.e. the restriction will be respected even if it makes the effective correction rate be different than the one computed according to the min/max rates and start/end blend values. Suggested values are greater than zero and smaller than PosBlendStart.

Note: as the factor is expressed in distance units (meters), it might need to be scaled proportionally to the overall scale of objects in the scene and speeds at which they move, which are factors that affect the expected magnitude of prediction errors.

The value, in meters, that represents the magnitude of the accumulated position error above which the error will be instantaneously corrected, effectively teleporting the rendered object to its correct position. Suggested values are greater than PosBlendEnd.

This setting has priority over the resultant correction rate, i.e. the restriction will be respected even if it makes the effective correction rate be different than the one computed according to the min/max rates and start/end blend values.

Note: as the factor is expressed in distance units (meters), it might need to be scaled proportionally to the overall scale of objects in the scene and speeds at which they move, which are factors that affect the expected magnitude of prediction errors.
The reference for the magnitude of the accumulated rotation error, in radians, at which the rotation error will be corrected at the MinRate. Suggested values are smaller than RotBlendEnd.

In other words, if the magnitude of the accumulated error is equal to or smaller than this threshold, it will be corrected at the MinRate. If, instead, the magnitude is between this threshold and RotBlendEnd, the error is corrected at a rate between MinRate and MaxRate, proportionally. If it is equal to or greater than RotBlendEnd, it will be corrected at the MaxRate.

The reference for the magnitude of the accumulated rotation error, in radians, at which the rotation error will be corrected at the MaxRate. Suggested values are greater than RotBlendStart and smaller than RotTeleportRadians.

In other words, if the magnitude of the accumulated error is equal to or greater than this threshold, it will be corrected at the MaxRate. If, instead, the magnitude is between RotBlendStart and this threshold, the error is corrected at a rate between MinRate and MaxRate, proportionally. If it is equal to or smaller than RotBlendStart, it will be corrected at the MinRate.

The value, in radians, that represents the magnitude of the accumulated rotation error above which the error will be instantaneously corrected, effectively teleporting the rendered object to its correct orientation. Suggested values are greater than RotBlendEnd.

This setting has priority over the resultant correction rate, i.e. the restriction will be respected even if it makes the effective correction rate be different than the one computed according to the min/max rates and start/end blend values.

member.name: T:Fusion.PlayerRef
member.summary: Represents a Fusion player.
member.para: The PlayerRef, in contrast to the player index, is 1-based. The reason is that default(PlayerRef) will return a "null/invalid" player ref struct for convenience. There are automatic cast operators that can cast an int into a PlayerRef.

example.code: default(PlayerRef), internally a 0, means NOBODY PlayerRef, internally 1, is the same as player index 0 PlayerRef, internally 2, is the same as player index 1

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.Comparer
member.summary: Gets an equality comparer that can be used to compare two PlayerRef instances.

member.name: F:Fusion.PlayerRef.MASTER_CLIENT_RAW
member.summary: A constant representing the raw index value for the master client.

member.name: F:Fusion.PlayerRef.INVALID_RAW
member.summary: A constant representing the raw index value for an invalid client. There are more values that'd result in being false.

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.Invalid
member.summary: Invalid player ref. Used to differentiate no player ref (None) from an invalid one.

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.None
member.summary: None player

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.InternalMasterClientIdentifier
member.summary: Special master client player ref value of -1. This is not a valid PlayerRef value in itself, and no Runner will ever be assigned this value as its LocalPlayer. It is used by properties like Object.StateAuthority to indicate that the MasterClient has authority To get the current master client PlayerRef use .

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.IsRealPlayer
member.summary: If this player ref is a valid unique player index

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.IsNone
member.summary: Returns true if the index value equals -1 (internal raw value of 0), indicating no player.

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.IsInternalMasterClientIdentifier
member.summary: Returns true if this PlayerRef indicates the MasterClient rather than a specific Player by Index, This is a special flag value which has the encoded index value of -2 (internal raw backing value of -1). This is not a valid PlayerRef value in itself, and no Runner will ever be assigned this value as its LocalPlayer. It is used by properties like Object.StateAuthority to indicate that the MasterClient has authority (which ever player that currently is), rather than a specific Player.

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.RawEncoded
member.summary:
Returns the index backing value without modification. Unlike which returns the backing value - 1.
0=None -1=MasterClient >0=PlayerId

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.AsIndex
member.summary:
Returns the PlayerRef int as an integer Id value.
-1=None -2=MasterClient >=0=PlayerId

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.PlayerId
member.summary:
Returns the PlayerRef as an integer Id value.
-1=None -2=MasterClient

member.name: P:Fusion.PlayerRef.IsInvalid
member.summary: Returns true if this PlayerRef has an invalid value.

summary.para: A circular buffer.
Normally, you push to the back and pop from the front. When it's full, PushBack will remove from the front and PushFront will remove from the back.

member.name: M:Fusion.SceneRef.FromPath(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a scene ref from a path. The most common use case for this method is when using Unity's addressable scenes. The path is hashed (31 bit), so on rare occasion there may be a hash collision. In such case consider renaming a scene or construct your own hash and use . To check if a scene ref is was created for a specific path, use .

member.returns: A SceneRef that represents the path.

member.name: T:Fusion.TickRate
member.summary: A tick rate is a collection of tick rates.

member.name: T:Fusion.TickRate.Selection
member.summary: Represents a selection of tick rates for client and server.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.Client
member.summary: The tick rate used by clients and the server.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ClientSendInterval
member.summary: The send interval for the client, used to calculate the Client Send Rate from the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ServerTickInterval
member.summary: The tick interval for the server, used to calculate the Server Tick Rate from the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ServerSendInterval
member.summary: The send interval for the server, used to calculate the Server Send Rate from the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ClientSendIndex
member.summary: The send tick rate for the client, as a ratio of the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ServerIndex
member.summary: Always equal to the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: F:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ServerSendIndex
member.summary: The send tick rate for the server, as a ratio of the Client Tick Rate.

member.name: M:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.ConvertObsoleteIndicesToIntervals
member.summary: If this selection is index-based (old format), converts the values of the obsolete index fields to intervals.

member.name: M:Fusion.TickRate.Selection.Validate
member.summary: Validates the tick rates in the Selection.
member.returns: A ValidateResult that indicates the result of the validation.
member.remarks:
The Selection is valid if:
- The client tick rate is within the valid range.
- The server tick interval in the Selection is a divisor of the client tick rate and is within the range of available intervals.
- The server send interval in the Selection is a divisor of the client tick rate and is within the range of available intervals.
- The client send interval in the Selection is a divisor of the client tick rate and is within the range of available intervals.
- The server send interval in the Selection is not smaller than the server tick interval.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkObject
member.summary: The primary Fusion component for networked GameObject entities. This stores the object's network identity and manages the object's state and input authority.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkObject.IsRenderInterpolationReady
member.summary:
Returns true when this object's interpolated state is usable. Intended for the remote-spawn hide/show pattern: hide in when this is false, show in once it becomes true, to avoid a brief visible stall.
Always true on the state authority, hiding and showing isn't required here as state authority spawned objects aren't interpolated
Note: Do not hide by calling gameObject.SetActive(false) on this NetworkObject's own GameObject (or any parent of it) in . Disabled GameObjects do not receive callbacks, so the show branch would never run and the object would stay hidden forever. Instead you could toggle a child GameObject or disable individual Renderers

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObject.EnableInterpolation
member.summary: Should the NetworkObject interpolate the network state? Disable it for performance improvement on objects that do not require interpolation, snapping it to the latest data. Changing this field after the object is spawned will have no effect.

member.summary: Returns true while a NetworkObject is valid (after it is spawned until it is despawned). While this property is true, Networked Properties and RPCs on the NetworkObject can be used. member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkObject.InputAuthority
member.summary: Returns the that has Input Authority over this network entity. PlayerRefs are assigned in order from 0 to MaxPlayers-1 and are re-used as players join and leave. The only caveat is that the server player (if one exists), always gets the last index no matter how many clients are connected.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkObject.StateAuthority
member.summary: Returns the that has State Authority over this network entity. PlayerRefs are assigned in order from 0 to MaxPlayers-1 and are re-used as players join and leave. The only caveat is that the server player (if one exists), always gets the last index no matter how many clients are connected.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkObject.IsSpawnable
member.summary: Toggles if this NetworkObject is included in the , which will include the prefab in builds as a Spawnable object.

member.summary: How Object Interest is determined for this object.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObject.Awake

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObject.SetPriority(Fusion.PlayerRef,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Sets the send priority of this object for .
Client/Server: must be called on the Server (Host or Dedicated); as it adjusts the rate at which the server sends data to the .
Shared Mode: must be the the request is forwarded to the plugin, which adjusts the send rate for this back to the
member.remarks: HostMigration: Priority is not replicated state and as such is not automatically carried over during a HostMigration, if your applicatiton supports host migratiton can be used to re-apply priorities on the new host.
member.returns:
true If called from an effective context e.g calling this from a Client in Client/Server will return false, calling this with the non-local player in SharedMode will return false
Note: This method asserts this in DEBUG builds when called in incorrect contexts

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObject.ClearPriority(Fusion.PlayerRef)
member.summary:
Clears a send priority previously set with for , restoring the default, calling this function is equivalent to calling SetPriority(player, 1)
Client/Server: must be called on the Server (Host or Dedicated); as it adjusts the rate at which the server sends data to the .
Shared Mode: must be the the request is forwarded to the plugin, which adjusts the send rate for this back to the
member.remarks: HostMigration: Priority is not replicated state and as such is not automatically carried over during a HostMigration, if your applicatiton supports host migratiton can be used to re-apply priorities on the new host.
member.returns:
true If called from an effective context e.g calling this from a Client in Client/Server will return false, calling this with the non-local player in SharedMode will return false
Note: This method asserts this in DEBUG builds when called in incorrect contexts

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags
member.summary: Enum representing the flags for network objects in the Fusion system. This enum is marked with the Flags attribute, allowing it to be treated as a bit field.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.None
member.summary: Represents a state where no flags are set.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.MaskVersion
member.summary: Mask for isolating the version part of the flags.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.V1
member.summary: Represents the first version of the network object flags.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.HasMainNetworkTRSP
member.summary: Has main TRSP?

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.Ignore
member.summary: Flag indicating that the network object should be ignored.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.MasterClientObject
member.summary: Shared Mode only. The current Master client always has State Authority for this object. If the Master client leaves the new Master client automatically becomes the State Authority for it.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.DestroyWhenStateAuthorityLeaves
member.summary: Should the NetworkObject be destroyed when the StateAuthority leaves?

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.AllowStateAuthorityOverride
member.summary:
State Authority Override allows clients to RequestStateAuthority over this object at any time even when another client has StateAuthority over the object. When this flag is set StateAuthority cannot be released by calling ReleaseStateAuthority.
Without this flag set RequestStateAuthority only works on objects with no StateAuthority. Without this flag set, to obtain StateAuthority over an object that already has a StateAuthority it is necessary to message (RPC) the current StateAuthority and have it call ReleaseStateAuthority and only once the StateAuthority has been reset to none call RequestStateAuthority.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectFlags.EnableAreaOfInterest
member.summary: Does the object has support Area of Interest?

member.name: T:Fusion.INetworkObjectProvider
member.summary: Interface which defines the handlers for Spawn() and Despawn() actions. Passing an instance of this interface to as the argument value will assign that instance as the handler for runner Spawn() and Despawn() actions. By default (if == null), actions will use Instantiate(), and Despawn() actions will use Destroy().

member.name: M:Fusion.INetworkObjectProvider.AcquireInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObjectAcquireContext@,Fusion.NetworkObject@)
member.summary: Acquires an instance of a prefab for a network object.
member.param: The NetworkRunner that manages the network objects.
The context that provides information for acquiring the prefab instance.
The acquired NetworkObject instance.
member.returns: A NetworkObjectAcquireResult indicating the result of the operation.

member.summary: Obsolete, use AcquireInstance((NetworkRunner runner, in NetworkObjectAcquireContext context, out NetworkObject result) instead.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkRunner
member.summary: Represents a Server or Client Simulation. IMPORTANT: You can only use a NetworkRunner once. Once that NetworkRunner disconnects from a game session or fails to connect it should be destroyed, and a new Network Runner instance should be created to start any new game sessions. For more information see the Online Manual.

members.member:
This callback is used to signal that the NetworkRunner has disconnected from the Photon Cloud, which is handled internally, either by the quick re-join or move to another room. It is useful to signal to the player that the connection is being reestablished, for example.
NetworkRunner instance Shutdown reason for the connection loss Flag to signal if the peer is attempting to reconnect to the Photon Cloud Online Manual.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkRunner.RemoteRenderTime
member.summary: The current time (current State.Time + Simulation.DeltaTime) for non-predicted objects (objects in a remote time frame). Use as an equivalent to Unity's Time.time. Time is relative to Tick 0 (which represents Time 0f).

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkRunner.LocalAddress
member.summary: Bound Address of the internal socket used for direct connection in Server/Host Mode. On the Host/Server this address contains the address/port that was passed into StartGame. When forwarding ports on the router/ firewall this port should be used as the internal port for the forwarding. The external port can either be the same value or any port. Note: This is not the public address of the server or the address / port that was discovered via NAT Punchthrough.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRunner.Attach(Fusion.NetworkObject,System.Nullable{Fusion.PlayerRef},System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Attaches a user-created network object to the network.
member.param: The network object to attach. Must not be null and must have a valid NetworkTypeId.
Optional PlayerRef. If assigned, it will be the default input authority for this object.
Optional boolean. If true, the object will be allocated in memory and attached to the scene object. Default is true.
Optional boolean. If provided, it will override the master client object setting. Default is null.
member.exception: Thrown when the provided network object is null.
Thrown when the provided network object has an invalid NetworkTypeId.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRunner.AddPlayerAreaOfInterest(Fusion.PlayerRef,UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single)
member.summary: Call this every FixedUpdateNetwork to add an area of interest for a player. Should only be called from the Host/Server in Server client mode. Should only be called for the local player in shared mode. The visible area for the player is not 1:1 to the AreaOfInterest. Interest is calculated using a grid based system that partitions the world into cells. All cells that touch the area of interest cube of the player are visible.
member.param: In Host/Server Mode the player for which to apply the area. In Shared Mode only
The center of the interest area.
The radius that determines the size of the interest area. The area is a cube not a sphere. The radius is half the size of the cube's edges.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRunner.ClearPlayerAreaOfInterest(Fusion.PlayerRef)
member.summary: Clears the area of interest for a player. This can only be called from the server/host

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunner._sceneInfoInitial
member.summary:
The initial scene info that was used to start the runner. It is consumed before the first tick by the scene authority.
The way this works is a bit complex. The order of things is as follows: first update -> BeforeFirstTick -> OnServerStart / OnClientStart (master) -> ConsumeInitialSceneInfo
Only then the initial scene info is consumed and is invoked. Any load/unload calls before that get queued and executed after the initial scene info is consumed.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunner._sceneInfoChangeSource
member.summary: What caused the scene change.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunner._sceneInfoSnapshot
member.summary: Last used scene info. It is compared to the current scene info and scenes are loaded/unloaded accordingly.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunner._sceneLoadInitialTCS
member.summary: Completed and nullified once the initial / first remote scene info is consumed. Loads/Unloads that happen before the first tick depend on the result of this task.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.PeerModes
member.summary: Options for running one or multiple peers in one Unity instance. Multiple is useful for testing multiple players/clients inside of the Unity editor without needing to build executables. Each peer is assigned its own independent physics scene and instance.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.PeerModes.Single
member.summary: This is the normal use case, where every build and the editor run a single server, host or client peer.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.PeerModes.Multiple
member.summary: This is the optional use case, which allows running multiple peers in the Unity editor, or in a build.

summary.para: State replication options for .
Interest Management enables NetworkObject Area Of Interest and Explicit Interest features.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.ReplicationFeatures.None
member.summary: No special replication handling. This setting is simplest, but does not allow using Interest to reduce bandwidth.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.ReplicationFeatures.InterestManagement
member.summary: Interest Management features are also enabled (Area Of Interest and Explicit Interest), allows more management of bandwidth.

member.summary: Render will always be invoked.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.PeerMode
member.summary: Setting for whether multiple peers can run per Unity instance (typically to allow easy testing of multiple peers inside of the editor).

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.LagCompensation
member.summary: Advanced lag compensation buffer settings.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.PhysicsForecast
member.summary: Global Configuration for NetworkTransform Forecast Physics. This must be set to true to allow any Forecast Physics extrapolation. When set to false Physics is synchronised to all proxies in remote time instead. When true Forecast Physics is controlled per Network Transform by

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.DoNotSetForecastDisabledProxyRigidbodiesToKinematic
member.summary: When true, the rigidbodys for proxy objects will not be set to kinematic when Forecast Physics is disabled. Setting this to false is for compatibility with Fusion 2.0 behaviour and is otherwise not recommended.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.EnqueueIncompleteSynchronousSpawns
member.summary: This flag changes the behaviour of NetworkRunner.Spawn to return null (instead of throwing an exception) and NetworkRunner.TrySpawn to return NetworkSpawnStatus.Queued if Fusion was unable to load a prefab synchronously (e.g. because it was Addressable). Fusion will enqueue the spawn and attempt to perform it the next frame, until successful. Useful for transition from Fusion 1.x.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.InvokeRenderMode
member.summary: Signal if the callbacks should be invoked.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.NetworkIdIsObjectName
member.summary: Signal if the of the should be included on the name of the GameObject

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.HideNetworkObjectInactivityGuard
member.summary: Inactive need special handling in case they get destroyed without ever being activated. This is achieved with adding a nested GameObject called "NetworkObjectInactivityGuard" that tracks the OnDestroy message. can be used to control whether these guards are visible in the hierarchy or not.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.AllowClientServerModesInWebGL
member.summary: Allow the use of Client-Server modes in WebGL builds. This is not recommended, as it can lead to degraded performance. Client-Server modes are not supported in WebGL builds by default, this setting allows its usage despite the limitations. Read more about the limitations of WebGL builds in the Fusion documentation at https://doc.photonengine.com/fusion/v2/fusion-intro.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.AllowMasterClientReassignRequest
member.summary: Allows for non-master clients to request to be elected the new master client.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.MasterClientRequestReassignThreshold
member.summary: Threshold time, in seconds, that the current master client must be without responding before other clients can request to be promoted to master.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.ClientsRecordFrameAndPacketTimingTraces
member.summary: When this setting is enabled, each client will record its frame and packet timing information to a local file (one file per session). Timing issues depend on many external factors, which makes them difficult to intentionally reproduce. But if an issue can be captured once, its trace can be used to simulate and troubleshoot it more effectively.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkProjectConfig.CheckRpcAttributeUsage

member.name: T:Fusion.GameMode
member.summary:
Fusion Game Mode.
Used to select how the local simulation will act.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.Single
member.summary: Single Player Mode: it works very similar to Mode, but don't accept any connections.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.Shared
member.summary: Shared Mode: starts a Game Client, which will connect to a Game Server running in the Photon Cloud using the Fusion Plugin.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.Server
member.summary: Server Mode: starts a Dedicated Game Server with no local player.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.Host
member.summary: Host Mode: starts a Game Server and allows a local player.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.Client
member.summary: Client Mode: starts a Game Client, which will connect to a peer in either or Modes.

member.name: F:Fusion.GameMode.AutoHostOrClient
member.summary: Automatically start as Host or Client. The first peer to connect to a room will be started as a Host, all others will connect as clients.

member.name: T:Fusion.StartGameArgs
member.summary:
Fusion Start Arguments, used to configure the simulation mode and other settings
More about matchmaking: https://doc.photonengine.com/en-us/fusion/current/manual/matchmaking

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.GameMode
member.summary: in which this peer will start

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.SessionName
member.summary:
Photon Cloud Session Name used either to Create or Join a Session.
Default: null (random session matching)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.SessionNameGenerator
member.summary:
Used to generate a new Session Name when creating a Session.
Default: null (a random session name is generated based on a GUID)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.Address
member.summary:
Peer Binding Address
Default:

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.CustomPublicAddress
member.summary:
Custom Public Reflexive Address
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.ObjectProvider
member.summary:
Object pool to use
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.SceneManager
member.summary:
See .
Default: null
More about Scene Loading: https://doc.photonengine.com/en-us/fusion/current/manual/scene-loading

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.Updater
member.summary: See

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.ObjectInitializer
member.summary: See

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.Config
member.summary:
Custom used to start the simulation
Default: Global NetworkProjectConfig
More about NetworkProjectConfig: https://doc.photonengine.com/en-us/fusion/current/manual/network-project-config

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.PlayerCount
member.summary:
Number of players allowed to connect to the session. Overrides the value set in the NetworkProjectConfig. The 1-255 range is purely a limit in the NetworkProjectConfig inspector the player count can be set to any positive int value via code.
Default: DefaultPlayers from the Global NetworkProjectConfig

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.Scene
member.summary:
Scene that will be set as the starting Scene.
Default: null (no scene set)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.OnGameStarted
member.summary:
Callback that is invoked when the Fusion has fully started
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.DisableNATPunchthrough
member.summary:
Flag to disable the NAT Punchthrough implementation and connect only via Relay
Default: false

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.CustomCallbackInterfaces
member.summary:
User defined callback interfaces we will provide O(1) constant time lookup for
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.ConnectionToken
member.summary:
Connection token sent by client to server. Not used in shared mode.
Default: null (empty connection token)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.PlayerUniqueId
member.summary:
Player Unique ID
Unique identifier for the player in a Session. Allows the same player to join a Session multiple times with the same .
Default: 0 (empty unique id)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.SessionProperties
member.summary:
Custom Session Properties. This dictionary can be used to either setup the initial Session Properties when creating a Session but also to set the matchmaking filters when joining a Random Session.
Default: null (empty custom properties)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.IsOpen
member.summary:
Session should be created Open or Closed to accept joins
Default: true

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.IsVisible
member.summary:
Session should be Visible or not in the Session Lobby list
Default: true

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.MatchmakingMode
member.summary:
Session Join Matchmaking Mode when joining a Session.
Default:

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.CustomLobbyName
member.summary:
Session Custom Lobby to be published in
Default: null (default Lobby for each Session Type, LobbyClientServer or LobbyShared)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.CustomSTUNServer
member.summary:
Specify a Custom STUN Server used to Resolve the peer Reflexive Addresses It can be used to specify multiple STUN Servers separated by semicolon (;)
Default: null (no custom STUN Server)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.AuthValues
member.summary:
Custom Authentication Data
Default: null (default authentication values)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.CustomPhotonAppSettings
member.summary:
Custom Photon Application Settings
Default: null (Global PhotonAppSettings)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.RealtimeClient
member.summary:
External Realtime Client to be used for communication with the Photon Cloud
Default: null (Fusion will create and handle the client)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.EnableClientSessionCreation
member.summary:
Enables the Session creation when starting a Client with an specific Session Name
Default: false (clients *can not* create new Sessions)

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.HostMigrationToken
member.summary:
Host Migration Token used when restarting the Fusion Simulation
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.HostMigrationResume
member.summary:
Callback invoked when the new Host is migrating from the old Host state
Default: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.StartGameCancellationToken
member.summary:
Optional CancellationToken used to cancel the NetworkRunner start up process and shutdown
Defaults: null

member.name: F:Fusion.StartGameArgs.UseCachedRegions
member.summary:
Enables the usage of the previous cached regions pings. This speeds up the region ping process and the runner startup process.
Defaults: true

member.summary: StartGameArgs ToString()

member.name: P:Fusion.Simulation.IsResimulation
member.summary: Use inside of FixedUpdateNetwork to determine if the tick currently being simulated has previously been simulated locally. Resimulation occurs in client prediction when new states arrive from the StateAuthority. Networked objects are set to the most current authority state tick, and simulations are repeated from that tick to the local current tick.

member.name: P:Fusion.Simulation.IsLastTick
member.summary:
Use in conjunction with IsResimulation/IsForward inside of FixedUpdateNetwork to determine if the current tick being simulated is the last tick of the resimulation or forward phase of the simulation loop.
'Resimulation' describes simulating a tick that has been previously been simulated.
'Forward' describes simulating a tick that is being simulated for the first time locally.
'Prediction' describes simulating ticks higher than the most current known StateAuthority snapshot tick.

member.name: P:Fusion.Simulation.IsFirstTick
member.summary:
Use in conjunction with IsResimulation/IsForward inside of FixedUpdateNetwork to determine if the current tick being simulated is the first tick of the resimulation or forward phase of the simulation loop.
'Resimulation' describes simulating a tick that has been previously been simulated.
'Forward' describes simulating a tick that is being simulated for the first time locally.
'Prediction' describes simulating ticks higher than the most current known StateAuthority snapshot tick.

member.name: P:Fusion.Simulation.IsForward
member.summary: Use inside of FixedUpdateNetwork to determine if the tick currently being simulated has NOT previously been simulated locally.

member.name: P:Fusion.Simulation.IsLocalPlayerFirstExecution
member.summary:
True if the current stage of the simulation loop is Forward. False during resimulations.
'Resimulation' describes simulating a tick that has been previously been simulated.
'Forward' describes simulating a tick that is being simulated for the first time locally.
'Prediction' describes simulating ticks higher than the most current known StateAuthority snapshot tick.

member.name: M:Fusion.Simulation.DrainPendingMessages
member.summary:
WARNING: This was intentionally extracted from the finally above and made NoInlining if this code is inlined or placed inside the finally block it can cause a SIGSEGV with no callstack if it iterates >2 times. Or cause it to rethrow a NulLReferenceException if iterated once. 0 iterations works fine.
This crash only manifests itself in Mono, using a release DLL when an exception is thrown in the try block. The cause is almost certainly a bug in the mono jit compilation of the try{}finally{} unwinding. Some analysis has been carried out of the jitted bytecode to back up this theory.
